data.title: American Express aims for 9% revenue growth this year, CEO says
data.description:
"We've been really good from a credit perspective," American Express (NYSE:AXP) Chairman and CEO Stephen J. Squeri said Tuesday at the Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services conference.

The company aims for 9% revenue growth this year, he noted. "10% is the correct aspiration."

"Spending has been relatively stable," he added.

An environment with not a lot of inflation and taxes would be helpful to increase corporate spending. He expects a better of "line of sight" with the new White House administration.

"Scale gives us lots of opportunities from a leverage perspective," Squeri said, noting that the company has been focusing on Millennials and Gen Z. "We continue to retain customers and to acquire new customers."

Lending needs to grow slightly more than billings, Squeri said. "I'm very confident we'll continue to grow at mid-teens (percentage) EPS."

For the last four or five quarters, AXP billings have been growing at about 6%, he said.

American Express (AXP) stock rose 0.2% at ~9:33 AM ET.

Updated at 9:34 AM ET: Spending at restaurants has been booming, Squeri said.

Breaking... check back for updates.

data.title: SolarMax Technology signs MOU for cryptocurrency solar projects
data.description:
• SolarMax Technology (NASDAQ:SMXT) entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with SOS Limited (NYSE:SOS) to develop large-scale solar power and battery storage facilities in Nevada, Texas and Florida to provide power for SOS’ blockchain and cryptocurrency operations.
  • The MOU contemplates the development of six separate facilities, each with a capacity of approximately 50 megawatts, for a combined total of approximately 300 megawatts.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. finalizes $6.1B+ Chips Act funding with Micron
data.description:
Update: Adds share movement and additional information from Department of Commerce release.

The U.S. Department of Commerce has finalized one of its largest awards to date with memory chipmaker Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), the White House said in a statement on Tuesday.

The Boise, Idaho-based company had announced a preliminary memorandum of terms with the Department in April for up to ~$6.14B in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act.

The $6.165B award finalized today will support the construction of two fabs in Clay, New York, and one fab in Boise, Idaho as part of Micron’s (MU) total $125B investment across both states over the next two decades.

The Department will disburse the funds based on Micron’s completion of project milestones.

Additionally, the commerce department has agreed to preliminary terms with Micron (MU) for an additional $275M investment to expand and modernize its Manassas, Virginia facility and onshore a critical technology relied upon by the country’s defense industry, automotive sector, and national security community.

The expected capital expenditure for the modernization will be $2B over the next several years.

“These investments will help the U.S. grow its share of advanced memory manufacturing from nearly 0% today to 10% over the next decade,” Vice President Kamala Harris said.

The CHIPS Act provides for $39B in grants, billions more in loans and 25% tax credits to boost domestic chip manufacturing and reduce reliance on Asia.

Shares of Micron Technology (MU) rose 2.6% premarket on Tuesday.

The commerce department has announced preliminary deals with more than 20 companies and has finalized agreements with some companies, including Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Intel (INTC), GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Entegris (ENTG). The Biden administration is racing to get the deals finalized before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.

data.title: Sandstorm Gold renews credit facility and enters automatic share purchase plan
data.description:
• Sandstorm Gold (NYSE:SAND) entered into an automatic share purchase plan with its designated broker to facilitate the purchase of the company's shares under its previously announced normal course issuer bid.
  • The ASPP will facilitate purchases under the NCIB at times when the company would ordinarily not be permitted to make purchases, whether due to regulatory restriction or customary self-imposed blackout periods.
  • The company has renewed its revolving credit agreement allowing the company to borrow up to $625M. The amounts drawn on the facility are subject to interest at SOFR plus 1.75%–2.75% per annum.
  • The revised interest rates above SOFR represents a 75-basis point reduction at the upper end and a reduction of 12.5 basis points at the lower end when compared to the previous credit agreement.
  • The facility has a term of four years, maturing in December 2028.

data.title: Broadcom in focus as Citi reiterates Buy, ups target, ahead of Q4 results
data.description:
• Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) was in focus on Tuesday as Citi reiterated its Buy rating and upped its price target on the semiconductor and software giant ahead of its fiscal fourth-quarter results, slated to be released after the close of trading on Thursday.
  • Shares were down fractionally in premarket trading.
  • "We expect the company to report results above consensus driven by a recovery in the non-AI semiconductor business (35% of F24E sales) with a better-than-feared gross margin outlook due to higher software mix," analyst Christopher Danely wrote in a note to clients. "However, we believe F1Q25 guidance will be tempered as there appears to be a slowdown in orders from leading AI customer Google. We do expect business to ramp from Meta throughout C25 and pick up the slack." Danely raised his price target to $205 from $175 ahead of the results.
  • A consensus of analysts expect Broadcom to earn $1.39 per share on $14.06B in revenue for the coming quarter.

data.title: Borr Drilling posts $58M jackup deal in West Africa
data.description:
Borr Drilling (NYSE:BORR) +2.7% pre-market Tuesday after saying it won a new contract for its premium jackup rig Norve in West Africa by an undisclosed repeat customer.

The rig will go on contract at a ~$181,250 dayrate for a 320-day assignment, starting in Q3 2025, according to UpstreamOnline.com.

The contract is for five wells firm, plus up to five optional wells at mutually agreed prices.

Borr (BORR) said the contract adds $58M in contract revenue backlog, excluding options, mobilization and demobilization compensation.

The Norve rig is currently working for BW Energy in Gabon and is expected to wrap up its deal in February, followed by work with Marathon Oil from February to June next year.

data.title: Norwegian Cruise Line earns upgrade at Goldman as bullish industry trends lifts all boats
data.description:
Shares of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NYSE:NCLH) are getting a lift on Tuesday as the bullish outlook for the cruise industry earns the company an upgrade from Goldman Sachs to Buy from Neutral, aligning it with competitors Carnival Corporation (CCL), and Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL).

Goldman's Lizzie Dove sees multi-year pricing tailwinds generated by a number of factors, including: cruise demand outpacing the relatively benign supply setup in the industry; the “halo effect” from new ship launches; less discounting need to fill ships; and future land investments.

“Each of these companies is set up for significant balance sheet repair over the coming years, which should drive a continued re-rating higher from here and lead to increased interest from a broader investor base,” Dove said in Tuesday’s research note.

In tandem with her upgrade, Dove lifts her target price for NCLH by 21% to $35, and reaffirms her Buy rating for Carnival Corporation (CCL), and Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL).

Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) shares are up 3% into Tuesday’s regular open, giving a lift to Carnival (CCL), and Royal Caribbean (RCL).

data.title: Kopin regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Kopin (NASDAQ:KOPN) received a letter from Nasdaq stating that it has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price for continued listing.
  • Nasdaq had determined that for 10 consecutive business days, the closing bid price of the Kopin’s common stock has been at $1.00 per share or greater.

data.title: Viasat awarded up to $568M IDIQ contract from GSA
data.description:
• Viasat (NASDAQ:VSAT) on Tuesday said it has received a five-year, sole-source Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract through the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) with a $568 million ceiling.
  • Under the award, Viasat will support the migration of command, control, communications, computers, combat systems, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C5ISR) capabilities and best practices from Special Operations Forces to General Purpose Forces.
  • VSAT +2.72% premarket to $9.79.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Tuesday: MDB, BA and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Tuesday were largely flat, as market participants awaited some key inflation data later in the week. Here are some stocks to watch on Tuesday:

  • Class A shares of MongoDB (MDB) slipped more than 7% in pre-market trading, reversing an after hours gain following its FQ3 2025 results. The developer data platform delivered a top- and bottom-line beat and lifted its annual revenue and adjusted profit guidance. Revenue from MongoDB's (MDB) flagship Atlas database surged 26% Y/Y to comprise 68% of total quarterly revenue. The company also said its finance chief Michael Gordon would step down at the end of the fiscal year.
  • Boeing (BA) stock added nearly 2% ahead of the opening bell, after Reuters reported that the troubled planemaker had restarted production of its best-selling 737 MAX jet last week, citing three sources familiar with the matter. Boeing's (BA) production had been stopped amid a seven-week strike by more than 30K factory workers. The damaging strike ended in early November. According to Reuters, work on the 737 MAX plane started on Friday.
  • Shares of Alaska Air (ALK) jumped almost 10% ahead of market open, after the U.S. carrier introduced a new "three-year strategic plan" which it said would deliver $1B in incremental profit. Alaska Air (ALK) in mid-September completed its acquisition of Hawaiian Holdings. The company said it is targeting earnings per share of at least $10 for 2027. Additionally, it said it would launch a new global gateway from Seattle, Wash.
  • Micron Technology (MU) stock climbed nearly 3% in pre-market trading, after the White House said it had finalized a $6.1B investment with the U.S. memory chipmaker to support the construction of several chip facilities. According to Vice President Kamala Harris, Micron's (MU) total $125B investment over the next few decades will help the U.S. grow its share of advance memory manufacturing from nearly 0% currently to 10% over the next ten years.
